2605.05025.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Large Language Models via Internal Attention Divergence Signals
2605.05025.paper_summary: We propose a lightweight and single-pass uncertainty quantification method for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models. The method uses attention matrices to estimate uncertainty without requiring repeated sampling or external models. Specifically, we measure the Kullback-Leibler divergence between each attention head's distribution and a uniform reference distribution, and use these features in a logistic regression probe. Across multiple datasets, task types, and model families, attention divergence is highly predictive of answer correctness and performs competitively with existing uncertainty estimation methods. We find that this signal is concentrated in middle layers and on factual tokens such as named entities and numbers, suggesting that attention dynamics provides an efficient and interpretable white-box signal of model uncertainty.
2605.05025.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種輕量級且單次通過的不確定性量化方法，以檢測大型語言模型中的幻覺。<lb/>該方法使用注意力矩陣來估算不確定性，而無需重複取樣或外部模型。<lb/>具體而言，我們測量每個注意力頭的分佈與均勻參考分佈之間的 Kullback-Leibler 散度，並在邏輯回歸探針中使用這些特徵。<lb/>在多個數據集、任務類型和模型系列中，注意力散度對答案正確性具有高度預測性，並與現有的不確定性估計方法競爭表現。<lb/>我們發現這種信號集中在中間層以及事實性標記上，例如命名實體和數字，這表明注意力動態提供了一種高效且可解釋的白盒模型不確定性信號。
2605.05025.author: Gijs van Dijk et.al.
